nsw.name: New South Wales
nsw.procurementFramework: NSW Government Resource Efficiency Policy (GREP) and Procurement Board Direction PBD-2022-04 (Sustainable Procurement)
nsw.industryMix: Largest state economy with broad industry mix: financial services, professional services, retail, agriculture (Riverina, Murray, New England), construction, manufacturing, tourism. Hunter Valley coal and wine; Western Sydney logistics and light manufacturing.
keyRegulators: NSW Department of Planning, Industry and Environment
nsw.supplierContext: NSW Government procurement now embeds sustainability outcomes in all contracts over A$7.5m, with the Premier's Sustainability Procurement Strategy requiring disclosure of emissions, modern slavery and circular economy data.

vic.name: Victoria
vic.procurementFramework: Victorian Government Purchasing Board policies including the Social Procurement Framework and Climate Change Act 2017 disclosure obligations
vic.industryMix: Manufacturing centre (especially food processing, automotive supply chain, advanced manufacturing); agriculture in Goulburn Valley, Mallee and Gippsland; financial services in Melbourne; tourism and education.
keyRegulators: Department of Energy, Environment and Climate Action (DEECA)
vic.supplierContext: Victoria's Climate Change Act 2017 creates statutory emissions reduction targets that flow through into government procurement requirements. Local councils increasingly require Modern Slavery and emissions data in tenders.

qld.name: Queensland
qld.epaName: Queensland Department of Environment, Science and Innovation (DESI)
qld.procurementFramework: Queensland Procurement Policy with ethical supplier mandate and the Queensland Climate Action Plan 2030
qld.industryMix: Resources (coal in Bowen Basin, LNG, bauxite); agriculture (cane, beef, grains, horticulture in Darling Downs and Wide Bay); tourism (SEQ, Whitsundays, Cairns); construction; defence (Townsville, Toowoomba).
qld.supplierContext: Queensland's Climate Action Plan 2030 commits the state government to net-zero by 2050 with embedded supplier requirements. Coal-region supply chains face cascading Scope 3 requests from majors like BHP and Glencore.

wa.name: Western Australia
wa.epaName: Western Australian Environmental Protection Authority (EPA WA)
wa.procurementFramework: WA Procurement Reform with Aboriginal Procurement Policy and growing sustainability reporting expectations
wa.industryMix: Resources dominate: iron ore (Pilbara), gold (Kalgoorlie-Goldfields), LNG, lithium, alumina, nickel. Agriculture in Wheatbelt and South West. Construction servicing resource projects.
keyRegulators: Department of Water and Environmental Regulation (DWER)
Department of Mines, Industry Regulation and Safety
wa.supplierContext: WA's major resource companies (BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside) drive the most intense Scope 3 supplier data requests of any state — equipment, fuel, logistics and professional service suppliers face routine sustainability audits.

sa.name: South Australia
sa.epaName: Environment Protection Authority South Australia (EPA SA)
sa.procurementFramework: South Australian Industry Participation Policy with sustainable procurement guidelines
sa.industryMix: Wine (Barossa, McLaren Vale, Clare, Coonawarra); food processing; defence manufacturing (shipbuilding, advanced manufacturing); agriculture (cereals, livestock); renewable energy generation.
sa.supplierContext: SA's renewable energy leadership and defence procurement programmes drive supplier data requirements. Wine and food exporters face EU-side requests linked to CBAM and Farm-to-Fork strategy.

tas.name: Tasmania
tas.procurementFramework: Tasmanian Government Buy Local Policy with growing environmental disclosure requirements
tas.industryMix: Aquaculture (salmon especially); cool-climate horticulture and dairy; wine; forestry; tourism; renewable hydropower-based industry.
keyRegulators: Department of Natural Resources and Environment
tas.supplierContext: Tasmania's renewable energy base and clean-product positioning create export market demand for credible emissions data — particularly for seafood, dairy and wine destined for Asia and Europe.

nt.name: Northern Territory
nt.epaName: Northern Territory Environment Protection Authority (NT EPA)
nt.procurementFramework: Northern Territory Procurement Rules with Aboriginal Procurement Policy and emerging sustainability requirements
nt.industryMix: Mining (manganese, bauxite); gas (Bayu-Undan, Ichthys); cattle pastoral; horticulture (mangoes, melons); fishing; defence and government services.
keyRegulators: Department of Environment, Parks and Water Security
Department of Industry, Tourism and Trade
nt.supplierContext: NT pastoral and aquaculture exports increasingly require buyer-side emissions data. Major resource projects (Ichthys, Mount Isa-Cloncurry) cascade Scope 3 data requests to NT-based logistics, accommodation and equipment suppliers.

act.name: Australian Capital Territory
act.procurementFramework: ACT Government Procurement Policy and ACT Climate Strategy 2019–2025 supplier requirements
act.industryMix: Predominantly federal government and services; growing professional services, IT and university sector. ACT operates on 100% renewable electricity.
keyRegulators: Environment, Planning and Sustainable Development Directorate
act.supplierContext: Federal government procurement (concentrated in Canberra) embeds Modern Slavery and increasingly emissions requirements across all categories. Service providers to ACT and federal agencies face the most procurement-driven sustainability scrutiny in the country.
